## Annoying Sawfish Of Piety

### Game Description

Annoying Sawfish Of Piety is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their sawfish pieces on the board, trying to annoy their opponent by blocking their potential moves.

### Game Rules

#### Equipment

The game is played on a 5x5 square board.

#### Start Rules

The game starts with an empty board.

#### Play Rules

Players take turns placing their sawfish pieces on the board.

*   A player can place a sawfish piece on an empty square.
*   A player cannot place a sawfish piece on a square that is already occupied by another sawfish piece of the same player.
*   A player cannot place a sawfish piece on a square that would block their opponent's potential moves.

#### End Rules

The game ends when one player has no more valid moves left.

```ludax
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules

            (repeat (P1 P2)

                        (not
                            (and

                                    (adjacent

                                        direction:any

                                )
                                (exists

                                        empty

                                    )
                                )
                            )
                        )
                    )
                )

                (not

                                    direction:any

                                (adjacent
                                    empty
                                    direction:any
                                )
                            )
                        )
                    )
                )
                (mover win)
            )
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

The end rules state that the game ends when one player has no more valid moves left, and the game is won by the player who has valid moves remaining. If the board is full and no player has won, the game is a draw.

## Marvellous Hopping Booby Game Implementation

The Marvellous Hopping Booby game is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board of size 5x5. The objective of the game is to hop your booby to the opponent's end of the board.

### Game Rules

1. The game starts with each player's booby at their respective end of the board.
2. Players take turns hopping their booby to an adjacent empty square.
3. A player can only hop their booby to a square that is two spaces away in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) if there is an opponent's booby in the middle square.
4. If a player lands on a square occupied by an opponent's booby, they can capture that booby and move it to any empty square on the board.
5. The game ends when one player's booby reaches the opponent's end of the board. That player wins.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Marvellous Hopping Booby"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (5 5))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (or 
                            (adjacent empty)
                            (and 
                                (adjacent occupied opponent)
                                (adjacent empty)
                            )
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or 
                    (and (= (y mover) 5) (= (x mover) (any)))
                    (and (= (x mover) 1) (= (y mover) (any)))
                )
            ) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation

*   The game starts with each player's booby at their respective end of the board (1,1) for P1 and (5,5) for P2.
*   The play phase allows players to take turns hopping their booby to an adjacent empty square or to a square that is two spaces away if there is an opponent's booby in the middle square.
*   The result constraint ensures that the destination square is either empty and adjacent or empty and two spaces away with an opponent's booby in between.
*   The effects section allows for capturing an opponent's booby and incrementing the score.
*   The end phase checks if a player's booby has reached the opponent's end of the board. If so, that player wins. If the board is full, the game ends in a draw.

### Adding More Rules

To make the game more complex, we can add more rules such as:

*   Limiting the number of hops a player can make
*   Introducing obstacles on the board that cannot be landed on
*   Allowing players to move their booby in any direction (not just adjacent or two spaces away)

## Married Russet Inchworm Game Implementation

The Married Russet Inchworm game is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with two types of pieces: Russet and Inchworm. The objective of the game is to create a "married" pair of Russet and Inchworm pieces.

### Game Rules

1. The game is played on a 4x4 rectangular board.
2. Each player starts with two Russet and two Inchworm pieces placed on their respective sides of the board.
3. Players alternate turns.
4. On each turn, a player can move one piece to an adjacent empty space.
5. A Russet piece can "marry" an Inchworm piece by moving onto the same space as the Inchworm piece.
6. A player can only win by creating a married pair of Russet and Inchworm pieces.
7. The game ends when a player creates a married pair or when the board is full.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Married Russet Inchworm"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 4 4))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P1 (1 2))
            (place P2 (3 1))
            (place P2 (3 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (exists (and (occupied mover) (adjacent (occupied opponent)))) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation

* The game is defined with two players and a 4x4 rectangular board.
* The `start` rules place two pieces for each player on their respective sides of the board.
* The `play` rules allow players to alternate turns, moving one piece to an adjacent empty space.
* The `end` rules check if a player has created a married pair by verifying if there is an occupied space adjacent to another occupied space of the opponent. If so, the player wins. If the board is full and no player has won, the game ends in a draw.

These rules can be implemented by modifying the `play` rules to allow for different types of moves.

2. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board.
3. A player can only place a piece on an empty space that is adjacent to a space occupied by their opponent.
4. If a player has no valid moves, they must pass their turn.
5. The game ends when one player has no more valid moves or when the board is full.
6. The player with the most pieces on the board wins.

```lark
        (play

                                    (occupied P2)
                                )
                            )
                        )
                    )
                    (result

                            )
                        )

                )
                (force_pass)

        )

        )
    )
)
```

* The `result` constraint prevents a player from placing a piece if it would create a line of two pieces in a row.
* The `force_pass` section ensures that a player must pass their turn if they have no valid moves.
* The `end` section defines the game end conditions. The game ends when the board is full or when one player has more pieces on the board than their opponent.

// ---End rules---
end_rules: "(" "end" end_rule+ ")"
end_rule: "(" "if" super_predicate end_rule_result ")"
?end_rule_result: result_win | result_lose | result_draw | result_by_score

// -- Result definitions --
result_win: "(" mover_reference "win" ")"
result_lose: "(" mover_reference "lose" ")"
result_draw: "(" "draw" ")"
result_by_score: "(" "by_score" ")"

// -- Mask definitions --
super_mask: mask | super_mask_and | super_mask_or | super_mask_not
super_mask_and: "(" "and" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_or: "(" "or" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_not: "(" "not" super_mask ")"

mask: mask_adjacent
    | mask_center
    | mask_corners
    | mask_custodial
    | mask_edge
    | mask_empty
    | mask_occupied
    | mask_pattern
    | mask_prev_move

mask_adjacent: "(" "adjacent" super_mask direction_arg? ")"
mask_center: "center"
mask_corners: "corners"
mask_custodial: "(" "custodial" custodial_length_arg mover_reference? orientation_arg? ")"
mask_edge: "(" "edge" edge ")"
mask_empty: "empty"
mask_occupied: "occupied" | "(" "occupied" mover_reference ")"
mask_pattern: "(" "pattern" dimensions_arg pattern_arg rotate_arg? ")"
mask_prev_move: "(" "prev_move" mover_reference ")"

// "Multi-masks" are special keywords that are manually split into multiple
// sub-masks at compile time. This is mostly useful for the "connected" function,
// which expects a list of masks to check for connections between
multi_mask: multi_mask_corners
          | multi_mask_edges
          | multi_mask_edges_no_corners

multi_mask_corners: "corners"
multi_mask_edges: "edges"
multi_mask_edges_no_corners: "edgesNoCorners"

// ---Predicate definitions---
super_predicate: predicate | super_predicate_and | super_predicate_or | super_predicate_not
super_predicate_and: "(" "and" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_or: "(" "or" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_not: "(" "not" super_predicate ")"

predicate: predicate_equals
         | predicate_exists
         | predicate_full_board
         | predicate_function
         | predicate_greater_equals
         | predicate_less_equals
         | predicate_mover_is
         | predicate_passed

predicate_equals: "(" "=" function+ ")"
predicate_exists: "(" "exists" super_mask ")" // technically equivalent to (>= (count mask) 1) 
predicate_full_board: "(" "full_board" ")"
predicate_function: function // special syntax which is equivalent to "(>= function 1)"
predicate_greater_equals: "(" ">=" function function ")"
predicate_less_equals: "(" "<=" function function ")"
predicate_mover_is: "(" "mover_is" player_reference ")"
predicate_passed: "(" "passed" (mover_reference | BOTH) ")"

// Additional (potentially optional) arguments for predicates
custodial_length_arg: ANY | positive_int
dimensions_arg: "(" positive_int positive_int ")"
direction_arg: "direction:" direction
exact_arg: "exact:" boolean
increment_score_arg: "increment_score:" boolean
multi_mask_arg: multi_mask | "(" super_mask+ ")"
orientation_arg: "orientation:" orientation
pattern_arg: "(" positive_int+ ")"
rotate_arg: "rotate:" boolean

// Optional rendering details
rendering: "(" "rendering" rendering_detail+ ")"
rendering_detail: color_assignment

color_assignment: "(" "color" player_reference color ")"

// General-purpose definitions
?number: SIGNED_NUMBER
?positive_int: /[0-9]+/
?boolean: TRUE | FALSE
?edge: TOP | BOTTOM | LEFT | RIGHT | TOP_LEFT | TOP_RIGHT | BOTTOM_LEFT | BOTTOM_RIGHT
?direction: UP | DOWN | LEFT | RIGHT | UP_LEFT | UP_RIGHT | DOWN_LEFT | DOWN_RIGHT | VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?orientation: VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?color: WHITE | BLACK
// ----------------------------

?player_reference: P1 | P2
?mover_reference: MOVER | OPPONENT
name: STRING
variable_name: /\?[a-z][a-z0-9]*/
id: /[a-zA-Z0-9_]+/

// Constants
TOP: "top"
BOTTOM: "bottom"
UP: "up"
DOWN: "down"
LEFT: "left"
RIGHT: "right"
TOP_LEFT: "top_left"
TOP_RIGHT: "top_right"
BOTTOM_LEFT: "bottom_left"
BOTTOM_RIGHT: "bottom_right"
UP_LEFT: "up_left"
UP_RIGHT: "up_right"
DOWN_LEFT: "down_left"
DOWN_RIGHT: "down_right"
VERTICAL: "vertical"
HORIZONTAL: "horizontal"
ORTHOGONAL: "orthogonal"
DIAGONAL: "diagonal"
BACK_DIAGONAL: "back_diagonal"
FORWARD_DIAGONAL: "forward_diagonal"
ANY: "any"
TRUE: "true"
FALSE: "false"
MOVER: "mover"
OPPONENT: "opponent"
P1: "P1"
P2: "P2"
BOTH: "both"
WHITE: "white"
BLACK: "black"

// Lark imports
%import common.ESCAPED_STRING -> STRING
%import common.SIGNED_NUMBER
%import common.WS
%ignore WS


        example games:
        

(game "Complexity Demo" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Four"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 6 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination (and empty (or (edge bottom) (adjacent occupied direction:up)))))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Six"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 6) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Hex"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hex_rectangle 11 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 2) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 2) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Gomoku" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 15))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5 exact:true) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Pente" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination center))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Reversi" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (28 35))
            (place P2 (27 36))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (exists
                            (custodial any)
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)


(game "Tic-Tac-Toe" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 3))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalath" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hexagon 9))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play 
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover lose))
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalax" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 13))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (and 
                            (not (line 5))
                            (not (= (line 4) 1))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 4) 2) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
